title: Creative-environment brief (Design-0023 theme contract)
description: The ONLY artifact a model is permitted to emit. Everything downstream (placement, palette, camera spline, post-grade) is deterministic code. Enums are allowlists: an off-vocabulary value is REJECTED, never coerced into geometry. See docs/design/0023-creative-environment-pipeline.md.

schema.description: Brief contract version (SemVer-ish MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH). The two-disposer gate: every consumer (Blender now, Unreal later) MUST agree on MAJOR before disposing a brief. The validator rejects an unknown/incompatible MAJOR rather than silently mis-reading a contract it does not understand.

theme.description: Free text, prompt-derived. Coherence does NOT depend on it — the palette + code-placement do. It is metadata for the human + the lucid caption.

mood.description: Pipe-separated tags drawn from the mood allowlist. Maps to lighting + motion rate, deterministically.

palette.description: LOCKED. Code clamps every generated material's albedo/emission to the nearest entry here. Coherence is enforced here, not hoped for. 2..8 hex colours.

elements.description: WHAT to place, not where. Code computes every transform.

motion.description: Optional. The deterministic animation applied to an element by id. 'field' here names the element id that animates.

camera.description: INTENT only. Code lays the actual spline + keys the move. move/arc/easing are fixed vocab, never a free camera script.

subject.description: must reference an element id present in elements[]

path.description: ONE spline = visible motif (render_as) AND camera rail. from/to are abstract anchors code resolves to layout coordinates.

bindings.description: Live-interactivity intent. The KEY is a bindable target, the VALUE a desktop signal. Both are allowlisted. Inert in the film artifact; drives the shader uniform in ambient mode (Design-0023 Interactivity). The spike validates them but does not wire the live loop.
